policy: Full Fundstrat transcripts are stored only in the private source vault.

items.title: Interim Deal Underpins a Constructive Framework as Industrials Break Out and Semis Rebound
items.completeness_notes: Logged-in Fundstrat technical-strategy video detail page showed Vimeo Transcript control; complete player-exposed VTT caption track captured from the same iframe metadata.
items.short_synthesis: Newton stayed near-term bullish as lower WTI, yields, and USD improved the cross-asset backdrop, while flagging FOMC/gap-fill resistance and emphasizing Industrials breadth plus Semiconductor support levels.

items.title: Geopolitical Tailwinds May Give Warsh Room to Lean Dovish, HYPE Reclaims Leadership
items.completeness_notes: Logged-in Fundstrat video detail page showed Vimeo Transcript control; visible panel was virtualized, so the complete player-exposed VTT caption track was captured from the same iframe metadata.
items.short_synthesis: Farrell framed lower crude/yields after US-Iran progress as a better liquidity backdrop for crypto, while keeping Strategy reserve coverage as the key unfinished risk and noting HYPE leadership recovery.

items.title: DXY Trends Reinforce Favorable Odds of a Tactical Rally, but Durable Lows Remain Difficult to Confirm
items.completeness_notes: Logged-in Fundstrat detail page showed a Vimeo embed with caption/CC availability; complete player-exposed VTT caption track was captured from the same embed metadata. No separate transcript pane was visible in the Fundstrat page DOM.
items.short_synthesis: Farrell sees improved crypto risk/reward and tactical-rally odds, but says durable lows remain unconfirmed and STRC/MSTR reserve mechanics keep sleeve sizing tactical rather than aggressive.

items.title: Video: Macro Minute: Wed is the debut of Kevin Warsh as Fed Chair with a new 'limited comms' style. Also, SPCX IPO successful as we expected.
items.completeness_notes: Logged-in Fundstrat detail page showed a Vimeo embed with caption/CC availability; complete player-exposed VTT caption track was captured from the same embed metadata. No separate transcript pane was visible in the Fundstrat page DOM.
items.short_synthesis: Tom Lee treated Warsh first FOMC communication style as the main next-day macro checkpoint and framed the SPCX IPO as supportive of AI/private-market capital appetite, pending post-FOMC confirmation.

items.title: Warsh's First FOMC Lifts the Dollar and Rates, but the Pop Looks Exaggerated
items.completeness_notes: Logged-in Fundstrat detail page showed a Vimeo embed with caption/CC availability; complete player-exposed VTT caption track was captured from the same embed metadata. No separate transcript pane was visible in the Fundstrat page DOM.
items.short_synthesis: Newton keeps near-term U.S. equity trends bullish after Warsh FOMC, treats the dollar/rates pop as exaggerated, and frames the SPX pullback into Fibonacci support as buyable rather than a larger breakdown.

items.title: Video: Macro Minute: Warsh solid FOMC presser. Contrary to consensus, Fed is not hawkish, but moving to 'dynamically navigating' rates
items.completeness_notes: Chrome transcript pane was visible in logged-in Fundstrat Chrome. Captured 40 visible/previously exposed cues from 00:03 through 06:34 by opening the Vimeo transcript pane and keyboard-scrolling; possible minor omissions if the virtualized pane skipped hidden duplicate fragments.
items.short_synthesis: Tom Lee frames Warsh as not hawkish and as dynamically navigating rates, making the post-FOMC broad-equity dip a buyable/recheck setup rather than a de-risking signal.

items.title: Two Key Ways to Play the Rebound in the Consumer as Crude's Decline Continues
items.completeness_notes: Chrome/Vimeo captions file was exposed from the controlled player frame and captured via the observed text/vtt response: 134 cues through the 00:07:43 ending cue. This replaces the earlier scroll-window transcript with fuller caption-file text; no raw transcript is stored in public repo files.
items.short_synthesis: Newton framed the June 18 close as bullish broadening: lean bullish, add on weakness while June lows hold, favor consumer rebound via JETS/DAL and XHB, keep QQQ constructive above 722, and watch SMH overbought risk despite trend strength.

items.title: It's Time to Look for Opportunities in the Mag 7 Following the Weakness
items.completeness_notes: Chrome showed Vimeo Transcript control and English auto-generated captions; captured 122 VTT cues from the same visible player config after verifying the transcript pane in Chrome.
items.short_synthesis: Newton frames the Mag 7/QQQ pullback as an opportunity rather than a breakdown: breadth stayed muted, MAGS/QQQ shows weekly exhaustion, and June lows are the risk line for add exposure.

items.title: Video: Macro Minute: Markets still digesting Fed Warsh's presser, as yields up but oil down. We still see this as a 'buy the dip' market
items.completeness_notes: Logged-in Fundstrat Macro Minute detail page showed full companion article text plus Vimeo Transcript and CC/subtitles controls; complete player-exposed auto-generated VTT caption track was captured from the embedded Vimeo config.
items.short_synthesis: Tom Lee framed the stalled S&P near 7500 as digestion of Warsh and higher yields, not a broken tape, and kept a buy-the-dip stance because oil is lower and Warsh is viewed as dynamic rather than hawkish.

items.title: A Korean Shakeout and Silver, Copper Washouts Don't Warrant Fading the US Stock Rally
items.completeness_notes: Logged-in Fundstrat detail page showed full article text plus Vimeo Transcript and CC/subtitles controls; complete player-exposed auto-generated VTT caption track was captured from the same embed metadata after the visible transcript panel proved virtualized.
items.short_synthesis: Newton kept the US equity trend bullish after the Korea/China/metals washout, treating SPX/QQQ weakness as corrective at support while flagging MU/PCE, EWY/SOX, KIE, silver, and copper as the next rechecks.

items.title: Strategy Rebuilds Confidence, But Liquidity Conditions Remain a Pressing Question
items.completeness_notes: Logged-in Fundstrat detail page showed a Vimeo embed; player metadata exposed an English auto-generated VTT caption track. Captured the complete VTT cue text exposed by Chrome/Vimeo metadata; no separate Fundstrat transcript pane was visible in the page DOM.
items.short_synthesis: Farrell treats Strategy common-stock issuance and USD-reserve rebuilding as a confidence repair for MSTR/STRC, but keeps crypto posture tactical because STRC remains below par, reserve coverage is still short of the preferred 12-month threshold, and broader liquidity conditions remain the key unresolved risk.

items.title: Video: Macro Minute: Semis -7% 1D. This is the 18th time since 2011. 88% of the time, Semis more than recover 1M later. Buy that dip.
items.completeness_notes: Vimeo player config exposed English auto-generated VTT captions; captured 80 cues from the caption track.
items.short_synthesis: Tom Lee frames the sharp semiconductor and memory selloff as a buyable dip based on historical recoveries, while keeping Fed/oil/AI inflation framing as a macro check.

items.title: Stellar MU Earnings Should Help Fuel a QQQ Rebound Back to Highs
items.completeness_notes: Logged-in Fundstrat detail page showed full article text and Vimeo embed 1204354280; Vimeo player metadata exposed English auto-generated VTT captions. Captured 83 de-duplicated caption lines from the exposed caption track; article detail text was also visible.
items.short_synthesis: Newton kept near-term US equity trends bullish after QQQ held 704 support and MU beat after close, mapping QQQ upside triggers above 720/743 while flagging MU extension risk, Healthcare strength, and a JETS airline breakout.

items.title: Broad Risk Weakness and a Motivated Seller Push BTC Lower, MU Earnings Could Provide a Bounce to Miners
items.completeness_notes: Logged-in Fundstrat detail page showed article text and Vimeo video 1204382162; Vimeo player exposed Transcript control plus English auto-generated subtitle track, captured from Chrome-visible player VTT.
items.short_synthesis: Sean Farrell frames BTC's latest drop as broad risk weakness plus idiosyncratic selling pressure, while MU's earnings could create a tactical bounce in AI-linked miners; treat this as a miner re-check/watch, not broad crypto risk-on confirmation.

items.title: Video: Macro Minute: Raise S&P 500 target to 8,000 vs 7,700 prior - June 2026 Top Large Cap and SMID Cap Core Stock Ideas Market Update
items.completeness_notes: Logged-in Fundstrat detail page showed Vimeo embed 1204352959; Vimeo player metadata exposed English auto-generated VTT captions. Captured 84 de-duplicated caption lines from the exposed caption track; no separate Fundstrat transcript pane was visible.
items.short_synthesis: Tom Lee raised the 2026 S&P 500 year-end target to 8,000 from 7,700, tying the higher target to higher 2027 EPS despite a lower target P/E and flagging the June 25 macro/top-ideas webinar as the follow-up review point.

items.title: Raising My 2026 SPX Target to 8,000 from 7,300, Joining Tom Lee
items.completeness_notes: Logged-in Fundstrat Daily Technical Strategy detail page showed full article text and Vimeo embed 1204693397; Vimeo player exposed Transcript/CC controls plus English auto-generated VTT captions. Captured 164 de-duplicated caption lines from the Chrome-visible player track. Compact article-detail rows were already routed through web intake, so no duplicate compact rows were attached here.
items.short_synthesis: Mark Newton raised his 2026 SPX target to 8,000 from 7,300, framed the week's weakness as buyable consolidation, and emphasized breadth improvement outside mega-cap Tech while keeping Tech weakness on watch.

items.title: Video: Macro Minute: May Core PCE shows 'war inflation' probably peaked, hence, rates low. Semi and Memory dip is still buyable
items.completeness_notes: Logged-in Fundstrat Macro Minute detail page showed companion article text and Vimeo embed 1204691039; Vimeo player exposed Transcript/CC controls plus English auto-generated VTT captions. Captured 150 de-duplicated caption lines from the Chrome-visible player track.
items.short_synthesis: Tom Lee frames May Core PCE as close to the Fed target once financial-services inflation is stripped out, argues war inflation likely peaked as yields fall, and reiterates that the semi/memory dip remains buyable.

items.title: Lowering Technology to Neutral from Bullish on a Tactical Basis
items.completeness_notes: Vimeo transcript panel exposed in Chrome; captured 53 visible timestamped cues by scrolling transcript list. First 00:00 cue was not exposed in final de-duplicated capture; companion article text was also visible on the detail page.
items.short_synthesis: Newton tactically lowers Technology to Neutral, expects choppy tech/semi consolidation into mid-to-late July, favors Healthcare/defensive cyclicals near-term, and treats the larger market uptrend as intact after the pullback.

items.title: MSTR mNAV Below 1.0x Raises the Stakes for Monday's 8-K, Borrow/Lend and the SOL Ecosystem Show Early Signs of Life
items.completeness_notes: Vimeo transcript panel exposed in Chrome; captured 51 visible timestamped cues by scrolling transcript list. First 00:00 cue was not exposed in final de-duplicated capture; companion article text was also visible on the detail page.
items.short_synthesis: Farrell says MSTR below 1.0x mNAV makes Monday 8-K pivotal; reserve rebuild and STRC dividend discipline matter more than chasing BTC-per-share optics, while SOL/borrow-lend strength is selective rather than broad crypto confirmation.

items.title: Ongoing Rotation with Just Minor Evidence of Defensiveness
items.completeness_notes: Logged-in Fundstrat Daily Technical Strategy detail page showed full article text and Vimeo embed 1205596158; Vimeo player metadata exposed English (auto-generated) captions. Captured 110 de-duplicated caption lines from the Chrome-visible player track.
items.short_synthesis: Newton keeps US equities range-bound rather than broken: respect SPX negative-gamma volatility and 7,300 support, avoid pressing Tech/QQQ beta before confirmation, and watch selective non-Tech rotation without overstating defensiveness.

items.title: MSTR Increases Reserve to 17.4 Months of Dividends, Formally Makes BTC a Funding Source (This is Overall a Net Positive)
items.completeness_notes: Logged-in Fundstrat detail page showed full article text, a Vimeo Transcript control, and CC/subtitles. Captured complete player-exposed English auto-generated VTT caption track from Vimeo video 1205646991 after verifying controls in Chrome; 121 de-duplicated cues.
items.short_synthesis: Farrell frames Strategy's reserve rebuild and 12-month reserve policy as a net positive for the preferred complex, while common equity is roughly neutral, BTC has a possible funding-source overhang, and broader crypto may trade better if the Strategy-specific risk is neutralized.

items.title: Insurance Breaks Out Above a Two-Year Base, Warranting an Overweight within the Financial sector
items.completeness_notes: Captured Vimeo English auto-generated captions exposed in Chrome player; transcript button and caption track visible. VTT parsed by removing cue timings; auto-generated text may contain recognition errors.
items.short_synthesis: Newton keeps US equities range-bound rather than broken, warns not to press directional bets inside SPX 7300-7550 / QQQ 712-744 ranges, and upgrades Insurance/KIE leadership after a two-year base breakout.

items.title: Crypto Fails to Participate as Strategy Supply Overhang Persists, Circle’s Growth Assumptions Deserve a Reset (Portfolio Rebalance)
items.completeness_notes: Captured Vimeo English auto-generated captions exposed in Chrome player; transcript button and caption track visible. VTT parsed by removing cue timings; auto-generated text may contain recognition errors.
items.short_synthesis: Farrell moves crypto posture more defensive: CRCL removed from crypto equities, BTC cut 5% into USDC, cash raised to roughly 50% while Strategy supply and OpenUSD/Circle competition remain unresolved.

items.title: Video: Macro Minute: As 2Q26 comes to an end, equity markets still in good shape but NYSE margin is a further sign consolidation likely ahead
items.completeness_notes: Captured Vimeo English auto-generated captions exposed in Chrome player; transcript button and caption track visible. VTT parsed by removing cue timings; auto-generated text may contain recognition errors.
items.short_synthesis: Lee remains less hawkish than markets because Truflation is low and equities are still in good shape, but record May NYSE margin debt adds to the case for consolidation later in 2026.

items.title: Daily Technical Strategy: Technology's bifurcation isn't Bearish, as Mag 7 snaps back to life
items.completeness_notes: Logged-in Fundstrat detail page exposed full article text plus Vimeo Transcript/CC controls; complete English auto-generated caption track was available from the loaded player configuration. Transcript panel was virtualized in Chrome, so the full VTT caption track was used.
items.short_synthesis: Newton frames the July 1 technology bifurcation as not bearish: Mag 7 and software are snapping back while semis/memory remain weak; MAGS weekly confirmation vs RSP is the key near-term recheck, and EWY/semis need stabilization before re-engaging.

items.title: Macro Minute: As we look at 2H2026, 6 factors support why we are buying the dip
items.completeness_notes: Logged-in Fundstrat Macro Minute detail page exposed companion article text plus Vimeo Transcript/CC controls; complete English auto-generated caption track was available from the loaded player configuration.
items.short_synthesis: Lee keeps a constructive 2H2026 posture: stronger economy, downside inflation risk, and a Fed potentially more dovish than priced support buying dips, while margin debt remains a later-year consolidation risk.
